description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain pattern and a visibly ergonomic grip. The head of the axe is forged from dark, weathered metal, with a broad, rectangular blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end. The blade edge is straight and sharp, suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks, while the poll is squared off for striking. The handle is securely fitted into the eye of the axe head, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a traditional wedged mounting. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or branding visible. The axe shows signs of use, with minor surface wear on both the wood and metal.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, dark metal blade, designed for chopping and striking tasks. The tool has a utilitarian, well-used appearance with no visible branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad, rectangular metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow edge of the blade and the handle's alignment through the axe head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the handle's curve and the blade's rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view showing the handle's grain and the mounting of the blade, with the poll and edge visible.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and a traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, rectangular steel blade with a straight edge and flat poll, single cutting edge, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle fitted through the eye of the blade.
